name: native-icons
description: Show native OS file icons in Pulsar

enum.description: Support - overlay alongside another icon package
Service - register services as the primary icon source

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Pick `support` when running alongside another icon package like `file-icons`: no services are registered and only listed files get a native icon, everything else keeps the icon assigned by the other package. Pick `service` when `native-icons` is your only icon source: registers `file-icons.element-icons` and `atom.file-icons` services so the tree-view, tabs, archive-view, and other consumers receive icons from this package. Restart needed.

greenlist.title: Greenlist
greenlist.description: *Only support mode*. Apply native icons to filenames matching one of these CSS-compatible patterns. Service mode ignores this setting. Support mode requires a greenlist. Supported forms are `*.ext`, `*ext`, `name*`, `*sub*`, and `exact`, matched case-insensitively against `data-name`. Entries may contain comma-space separated patterns, e.g. `*.dat, *ipy`. Patterns containing `?` or `*` in the middle are ignored with a console warning.

blacklist.title: Blacklist
blacklist.description: *Only support mode*. Never apply native icons to filenames matching one of these CSS-compatible patterns. Service mode ignores this setting. Same syntax and comma-space separated entries as greenlist. Blacklist always wins. Examples: `*.min.js`, `*.bak`, `*~`.

useCustomFileTypes.title: Honour `core.customFileTypes`
useCustomFileTypes.description: When a file matches a pattern under Pulsar's `core.customFileTypes` (e.g. `source.python: ["ipy"]`), show the icon of the grammar's primary file extension (`.py`) instead of the file's real extension. Useful for custom extensions that share a grammar.

useFullPathForExecutables.title: Use real path for embedded-icon binaries
useFullPathForExecutables.description: For `.exe`, `.lnk`, `.ico`, `.dll`, `.url`, `.scr`, `.msi` files, query the actual file's icon (each binary carries its own). Turn off to use a generic icon per extension.
